title: potentilla arenaria subvar. epipsila
intro: Potentilla arenaria subvar. epipsila, also known as Beach Cinquefoil, is a perennial herb native to coastal areas, particularly in the North Atlantic region, characterized by its yellow flowers and five-petaled leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it useful for treating various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal problems. The health benefits of Beach Cinquefoil include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system, as well as its potential to act as an antiseptic and antiviral agent. Herbal preparations of Potentilla arenaria subvar. epipsila can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions.
